page: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 480 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This allocation represents a clear tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute density over memory capacity. For storage-heavy workloads, the server bundles 3780 GB of local NVMe storage and supports Premium Storage. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 67,973.49 and a PassMark CPU score of 83,685.33. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks achieve top-tier ratings, with gzip compression reaching 35.76 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.91 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is robust, registering 4,705.01 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 311.23 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

Given the high compute density and local storage capacity, the D64lds_v7 is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for high-throughput database operations, as evidenced by a PassMark database score of 31,743.02 and 109.21 million Redis SET operations per second. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and CPU-based machine learning inference, where it demonstrates strong prompt processing speeds for 7B and 70B models.
description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Featuring 128.0 GB of memory, it presents a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, trading memory capacity for high compute density. The server includes 3780 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but lacks GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 83,685.33 and top-tier single-threaded compression speeds. It delivers strong results in database operations and Redis SET performance, making it highly suitable for high-throughput web serving, database hosting, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v7 is a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon server with 128 GB RAM and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for high-compute workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-dense server featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and local storage for demanding database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz.
128.0 GB system memory, optimized for compute-dense workloads.
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier single-threaded compression and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
Excellent fit for high-throughput database operations and Redis caching.
Supports advanced vector and matrix instructions (AVX-512 and AMX).
